steps.name: the-pitch
steps.narration: This is Spool. When an agent ships a feature, it records a real walkthrough in the browser, narrates it, and hands you one link. No human ever hits record.

steps.name: how-it-works
steps.narration: Under the hood it is record first. The agent drives the app once at natural speed, narration generates in parallel, and the renderer fits every step to the voice.

steps.name: a-published-spool
steps.narration: Here is a published spool. A real continuous recording on a designed canvas, with word-synced captions baked in.

steps.name: chapters
steps.narration: Chapters come free from the step structure. Click one and the player jumps straight to that moment.

steps.name: transcript
steps.narration: The full transcript rides along, timestamped per step.

steps.name: receipts-for-agents
steps.narration: It is not just for humans. Every spool ships machine-readable receipts, so another agent can review the demo, verify what changed, and file bugs without watching a single frame.

steps.name: edited-from-the-web
steps.narration: One more thing. The video on this page was edited after publishing. We re-voiced the intro and cut a step from the site itself, and the cloud pipeline re-rendered it in three minutes. That is Spool today.
